description: You are a satellite ground station engineer at the University of Chile. Configure GPredict to track Earth Observation satellites from Santiago while accounting for the Andes mountains. Create a ground station named 'Santiago_UChile' at 33.4500° S, 70.6600° W, and 520 meters altitude, incorporating the horizon terrain profile from /home/ga/Documents/andes_mask.txt. Establish a tracking module named 'Andes_EO' bound to the 'Santiago_UChile' station. Include the satellites SUOMI NPP (NORAD 37849), FENGYUN 3A (NORAD 32958), and FENGYUN 3B (NORAD 37214) in the module, and configure the map settings to display 4 orbits for the ground track. Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

natural_language.prompt: Please configure our GPredict for the Santiago ground station. First, create a ground station called 'Santiago_UChile' at 33.45 South, 70.66 West, 520m altitude. Because of the Andes, we need you to go into its Horizon settings and import the mask file from /home/ga/Documents/andes_mask.txt. Next, create a module called 'Andes_EO' assigned specifically to this Santiago ground station. Add SUOMI NPP (37849), FENGYUN 3A (32958), and FENGYUN 3B (37214) to it. Finally, in the module's map configuration, set the ground track to show 4 orbits instead of the default 1.
